concerns.text: T031 runs Monte-Carlo validation (from T062) as part of pipeline start-up but explicitly states 'no explicit DEPENDS ON to avoid circularity'. This is a hard ordering violation: T031 cannot invoke T062's module if T062 hasn't been implemented yet. T031 must depend on T062 completion.

T028 (power analysis) depends on T025 (validator) but should logically precede T026 (synthetic dataset generation). Power analysis determines minimum N (FR-025), which should inform synthetic dataset size. Current ordering has T026 (10,000 samples) before T028 validates corpus size meets N≥300.

T018 (URL ingestion), T019 (HTML fetching), T020 (extraction) are all tagged [P] parallel-safe but have implicit data-flow dependencies: T019 needs T018's deduped URLs, T020 needs T019's fetched HTML. These cannot run in parallel despite [P] tags.

T096 writes checksums to data/checksums.txt while T076 writes to output/checksums.txt. This creates two checksum sources violating Constitution Principle IV (Single Source of Truth). One task should be removed or both should write to the same canonical location.

T069a sources ≥100 URLs with 'at least 20 per domain' but no verification task confirms the final real_world_labels.csv achieves this stratified distribution. Add explicit verification task for domain stratification compliance.
